selected_abstract.title: Lessons from Failed Active Learning: Scaffold Priors Dominate ML-Guided INVEST Discovery
selected_abstract.claim: We report a delta transfer learning active learning pipeline for INVEST molecule discovery in D-A heptazines that, despite achieving good retrospective LOO-CV metrics (MAE 0.078 eV, sign accuracy 84%), degrades substantially at deployment (MAE 0.10 eV, sign accuracy 60%). A rigorous closed-loop evaluation reveals that: (1) the deployed model does not significantly outperform uniform random selection on INVEST hit rate (7/15 vs expected 13/15 under the ΔDFT surrogate); (2) the Hz scaffold prior alone (67% INVEST rate, Clopper-Pearson 95% CI [0.30, 0.93]) accounts for all observed INVEST discoveries; (3) bootstrap ensemble uncertainty shows no correlation with actual errors (ρ = 0.10, p = 0.58). Nevertheless, the pipeline provides actionable value: post-round retraining improves LOO-CV MAE to 0.052 eV, and the diversity-driven selection confirms that 5AP derivatives are uniformly non-INVEST (5/5, all ΔEST > +0.17 eV), eliminating an entire scaffold family from further investigation. We argue that active learning's primary contribution in small-data molecular discovery is not prediction accuracy but information-efficient exploration that reduces uncertainty about the chemical space.
selected_abstract.scope_limits: CRITICAL CAVEATS: (i) Hz_NH23 (ΔEST = -0.38 eV) is a Grubbs outlier (z = -4.95) with zero oscillator strength and anomalously high T1; it requires DLPNO-CCSD(T) or CASSCF verification before any INVEST claim. (ii) All INVEST classifications are at RI-ADC(2)/def2-SVP level only — no molecule has been confirmed at ≥2 independent theory levels. (iii) The model's inability to distinguish INVEST from non-INVEST at deployment (sign accuracy = 60%) means it should not be used as a standalone screen. (iv) The ΔDFT calibrated surrogate predicts 88% pool INVEST rate, suggesting either systematic DFT bias or that most Hz derivatives are genuinely INVEST at ADC(2) level — in either case, the AL provides minimal lift over scaffold-aware random sampling.

A.title: Active Learning Accelerated Discovery of Inverted Singlet-Triplet Gap Molecules
A.claim: We demonstrate a delta transfer learning framework combined with multi-criteria active learning that achieves >75% sign accuracy in predicting INVEST character of novel D-A heptazine derivatives. A single round of 16 ADC(2) calculations, selected by our four-bucket acquisition function, discovers N new INVEST molecules with >60% recall, significantly outperforming random baselines (p < 0.05). Bootstrap ensemble uncertainty correctly ranks prediction difficulty (ρ > 0.4), enabling reliable prioritization of computationally expensive quantum chemical validation.
A.scope_limits: All results are for RI-ADC(2)/def2-SVP level of theory on heptazine-based donor-acceptor architectures. The INVEST classification is preliminary and requires multi-level confirmation.

B.title: Delta Transfer Learning for Efficient Screening of INVEST Candidates in Donor-Acceptor Heptazines
B.claim: We present a delta transfer learning approach that leverages 446 source-domain molecules to predict singlet-triplet gaps in 155 novel D-A heptazine derivatives. While the deployed model achieves moderate sign accuracy (60-75%) with MAE ~0.10 eV, active learning provides value through scaffold-diverse exploration: Round 1 confirms that Hz derivatives exhibit a ~67% INVEST rate while 5-aminopyrimidine derivatives are uniformly non-INVEST, a finding that saves substantial future compute. Post-round retraining with 15 new labels reduces LOO-CV MAE from 0.078 to 0.052 eV, demonstrating effective closed-loop learning.
B.scope_limits: The current model cannot reliably distinguish true INVEST from near-zero molecules (sign accuracy 60% at deployment). Bootstrap uncertainty does not correlate with actual prediction errors (ρ ≈ 0.1). Results should be interpreted as a computational exploration tool rather than a predictive screen.

C.title: Lessons from Failed Active Learning: Scaffold Priors Dominate ML-Guided INVEST Discovery
C.claim: We report a delta transfer learning active learning pipeline for INVEST molecule discovery in D-A heptazines that, despite achieving good retrospective LOO-CV metrics (MAE 0.078 eV, sign accuracy 84%), degrades substantially at deployment (MAE 0.10 eV, sign accuracy 60%). A rigorous closed-loop evaluation reveals that: (1) the deployed model does not significantly outperform uniform random selection on INVEST hit rate (7/15 vs expected 13/15 under the ΔDFT surrogate); (2) the Hz scaffold prior alone (67% INVEST rate, Clopper-Pearson 95% CI [0.30, 0.93]) accounts for all observed INVEST discoveries; (3) bootstrap ensemble uncertainty shows no correlation with actual errors (ρ = 0.10, p = 0.58). Nevertheless, the pipeline provides actionable value: post-round retraining improves LOO-CV MAE to 0.052 eV, and the diversity-driven selection confirms that 5AP derivatives are uniformly non-INVEST (5/5, all ΔEST > +0.17 eV), eliminating an entire scaffold family from further investigation. We argue that active learning's primary contribution in small-data molecular discovery is not prediction accuracy but information-efficient exploration that reduces uncertainty about the chemical space.
C.scope_limits: CRITICAL CAVEATS: (i) Hz_NH23 (ΔEST = -0.38 eV) is a Grubbs outlier (z = -4.95) with zero oscillator strength and anomalously high T1; it requires DLPNO-CCSD(T) or CASSCF verification before any INVEST claim. (ii) All INVEST classifications are at RI-ADC(2)/def2-SVP level only — no molecule has been confirmed at ≥2 independent theory levels. (iii) The model's inability to distinguish INVEST from non-INVEST at deployment (sign accuracy = 60%) means it should not be used as a standalone screen. (iv) The ΔDFT calibrated surrogate predicts 88% pool INVEST rate, suggesting either systematic DFT bias or that most Hz derivatives are genuinely INVEST at ADC(2) level — in either case, the AL provides minimal lift over scaffold-aware random sampling.
